# DBUI Design System
# Distribution URL: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/muditmittal/dbui/main/packages/dbui/llms.txt
#
# DBUI is Databricks' component library — 46 components, 451 icons, 162 tokens.
# Built on shadcn/ui + Base UI + Tailwind CSS v4, reskinned with DuBois design tokens.

## INSTALL — keep it LEAN

DBUI is UI code only. Do NOT pick a framework for the user. Do NOT install anything outside the list below.

### Prerequisite: a minimal React + Tailwind v4 project

- If the project already has `package.json` with React and Tailwind v4, continue to Step 1.
- If there is no `package.json`, STOP and ask the user. Suggest this minimal bootstrap, do NOT run it for them:
  ```bash
  npm create vite@latest . -- --template react-ts
  npm install tailwindcss @tailwindcss/vite
  ```

### DO NOT install any of these. They are not DBUI dependencies.

Heavyweight starters: `next-forge`, `create-t3-app`, `create-next-app --example …`, any Vercel template that auto-adds vendors.
Security / auth: **Arcjet**, Clerk, Auth0, NextAuth, Descope, Supabase Auth.
Databases / ORMs: Prisma, Drizzle, Convex, Supabase, Neon, Turso.
State / data libs: Redux, Zustand, Jotai, React Query, SWR, Apollo.
AI SDKs: Vercel AI SDK, LangChain, OpenAI SDK.
Icon packs: lucide-react, @heroicons/react, react-icons — DBUI already has 451 icons.
UI libs: shadcn CLI, Radix, Mantine, Chakra, MUI — DBUI replaces these.

If the user explicitly asks for one of the above later, fine. Never add any of them proactively.

### Step 1: Copy DBUI into the project

```bash
if [ ! -d "$HOME/dbui" ]; then
  git clone https://github.com/muditmittal/dbui.git ~/dbui
fi
cp -r ~/dbui/packages/dbui ./dbui
cp -r ~/dbui/packages/dbui-shells ./dbui-shells
cp ./dbui/CLAUDE.md ./CLAUDE.md
```

### Step 2: Add path aliases to `tsconfig.json`

```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "paths": {
      "dbui/*": ["./dbui/src/*"],
      "dbui-shells/*": ["./dbui-shells/src/*"]
    }
  }
}
```

Vite/Webpack projects: mirror these in `resolve.alias`.

### Step 3: Import DBUI tokens in root CSS

```css
@import "tailwindcss";
@import "./dbui/src/tokens/globals.css";
```

### Step 4: Install required deps — these four, nothing else

```bash
npm install @base-ui/react class-variance-authority clsx tailwind-merge
```

### Step 5: Install optional peer deps ONLY when needed

Add each one only when the user imports the matching component:

| Component(s) | Extra package |
|---|---|
| `Sonner` / `toast()` | `sonner next-themes` |
| `Drawer` | `vaul` |
| `Resizable` / `ResizablePanel` | `react-resizable-panels` |
| `Chart` / `chart.tsx` | `recharts` |

### Step 6: Create the first page with the Base Shell

```tsx
import { Base } from "dbui-shells"

export default function Home() {
  return (
    <Base defaultActive="catalog">
      <div style={{ display: "flex", alignItems: "center", justifyContent: "center", height: "100%" }}>
        <p style={{ fontSize: 13, color: "#6F6F6F" }}>DBUI is installed. Start building here.</p>
      </div>
    </Base>
  )
}
```

### Step 7: Start the dev server

```bash
npm run dev
```

### Update to latest

```bash
cd ~/dbui && git pull
cp -r ~/dbui/packages/dbui ./dbui
cp -r ~/dbui/packages/dbui-shells ./dbui-shells
cp ./dbui/CLAUDE.md ./CLAUDE.md
```

---

## WHERE TO LOOK — read these BEFORE writing any UI

These are the discovery layer. Each component's source file (`dbui/components/ui/<name>.tsx`) is the rules layer — read its `@guideline` and `@constraint` JSDoc when you actually use the component.

| Need | Canonical file |
|------|---------------|
| Pick the right component | `./dbui/docs/component-index.md` — searchable component table (category, when to use, avoid for, synonyms, Figma name) |
| Find the right icon | `./dbui/docs/icon-index.md` — searchable 449-icon index |
| Write any user-facing copy | `./dbui/docs/brandvoice.md` — vocabulary, tone, microcopy templates |
| Translate Figma ↔ React (component or inner-component names) | `./dbui/docs/figma-mapping.md` — top-level + inner components + slot encodings + edge cases |
| Pick a page-level layout / shell | `./dbui/composition.md` — five named shells with regions, scaling, scroll, primary action |
| Apply cross-cutting layout/spacing rules | `./dbui/docs/component-rules.md` — spacing rhythm, page padding, icon and button rules |
| Read full guidelines/constraints for a component | `./dbui/src/components/ui/<name>.tsx` — `@guideline` / `@constraint` JSDoc |
| Get entity icons for trees | `./dbui/src/components/icons/entity-icons.ts` — NEVER guess these |
| Design token values | `./dbui/src/tokens/globals.css` |

**Single source of truth:** per-component rules live ONLY in the component's JSDoc. Per-icon details live ONLY in `icon-index.md`. Cross-cutting layout rules live ONLY in `component-rules.md`. Shell-level composition lives ONLY in `composition.md`. Voice/tone lives ONLY in `brandvoice.md`. Figma ↔ React naming lives ONLY in `figma-mapping.md`. If you ever see a rule duplicated, the more specific source wins (JSDoc > index > llms.txt).

**Picking vs translating — don't mix them up:**
- *"Which component do I use for X?"* → `component-index.md`
- *"What's this Figma layer called in React?" / "Where does this React component live in Figma?"* → `figma-mapping.md`

---

## RULES (non-negotiable, cross-cutting)

These are global truths — every component obeys them. Per-component rules are in JSDoc.

1. ALWAYS use DBUI components. NEVER use raw `<button>`, `<input>`, `<div role="dialog">`.
2. ALWAYS use icons from `dbui/components/icons/{Name}`. NEVER install lucide, heroicons, etc.
3. ALWAYS use semantic tokens (`bg-primary`, `text-foreground`). NEVER hardcode hex colors.
4. ALWAYS import from `dbui/components/ui/{component}`. NEVER import @radix-ui or @base-ui directly.
5. Every page starts with `<Base>` from `dbui-shells`. NEVER build header/nav from scratch.
6. For hierarchical data, use `DataTreeView` or `FileTreeView`. NEVER fake trees with nested divs.
7. Base UI uses `render` prop, NOT Radix `asChild`:
   ```tsx
   <DialogTrigger render={<Button />}>Open</DialogTrigger>       // correct
   <DialogTrigger asChild><Button>Open</Button></DialogTrigger>   // WRONG — ignored by Base UI
   ```
8. Read `brandvoice.md` before writing any user-facing string. No emoji in product UI. No exclamation marks. No "utilize" / "leverage" / "seamless".

---

## ONE-LINE REMINDERS

These are facts agents forget most often. Full rules are in the canonical files in WHERE TO LOOK above — don't re-derive from these one-liners, look them up.

- **Base font is 13px, not 14px.** Body: `text-[13px] leading-[20px]`. Captions: `text-[12px] leading-[16px]`. Page title: `text-[22px] font-semibold`. Section heading: `text-[18px] font-semibold`. → full table in `docs/component-rules.md`.
- **Spacing rhythm: 8 / 16 / 24.** Inside a component: `gap-2`. Between form fields and sections: `gap-4`. Between major blocks: `gap-6`. → full table in `docs/component-rules.md`.
- **Surfaces.** Shell background: `bg-muted`. Content surface: `bg-background border rounded-md`. Page padding inside the surface: `px-6 py-4`. → `docs/component-rules.md` → "Platform Shell".
- **Three-column detail.** Tree (260px) · main (flex-1) · metadata sidebar (280px). Each scrolls independently. → `composition.md` → "Shell E".
- **Icons.** Categories are `object` / `action` / `indicator` / `component`. Always search `docs/icon-index.md` before inserting any icon. Never guess. Commonly confused: Experiments → `Beaker`, Jobs → `Workflows`, Compute → `Cloud`, Unity Catalog → `Catalog`, Genie → `SparkleDouble`, SQL Warehouse → `Database`, Marketplace → `Storefront`. → full table in `docs/icon-index.md`.

## KNOWN GAPS

Not yet available — compose from primitives as interim:
- DatePicker, Stepper, Banner, CodeBlock, Virtualized lists
